title: Interdependent Financial Relationships
description: Students learn how financial relationships work between entities in New Zealand, how money flows between them, and how events affect those relationships.
nceaStandardTitle: Demonstrate understanding of interdependent financial relationships
rationale: AS92030 is a Year 11 external standard worth 5 credits. Students must understand how two or more entities are financially connected, how money flows between them, and how events create flow-on effects. This module builds systematic thinking about financial interdependence using real NZ contexts including businesses, banks, government, households, and Māori organisations.
learningObjectives: Identify and describe financial relationships between two or more NZ entities
Describe how money flows between entities in a financial relationship
Describe how a specific event affects the financial relationship between entities
Explain how an event affects each entity and the flow-on effects of that event
Analyse the extent to which entities are interdependent and how each could respond to flow-on effects
assessmentTask: Students select two or more NZ entities with a financial relationship and write a structured analysis. The response must identify and describe the financial relationship, describe how money flows between the entities, describe how a specific event affects the relationship, and at Merit and Excellence levels explain the flow-on effects for each entity and analyse the extent of interdependence and how each entity could respond.

lessons.title: Excellence Skills — Extent of Interdependence and Response Strategies
Bringing It All Together — Full Analysis and Common Mistakes
